decisions.briefing: Markets are in risk-off mode today with NASDAQ dropping 1.3% and the S&P 500 down 0.7%, and the screener returned zero candidates — a signal to stay flat. Holding all five current positions and letting the automated stop-losses do their job if any position deteriorates. Cash stays on the sidelines until the market stabilizes and quality candidates re-emerge.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities sold off broadly on April 28 as investors rotated out of risk assets amid renewed concerns over sticky inflation data and uncertain Fed policy timing. Technology stocks led the decline, with the NASDAQ falling 1.3%, as higher-for-longer rate expectations weighed on growth valuations. Defensive sectors held up better, reflecting classic risk-off behavior where capital moves toward safety rather than growth.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市於4月28日全線下跌，投資者因通脹數據持續偏高及聯儲局政策前景不明朗而轉向避險。科技股首當其衝，納斯達克指數重挫1.3%，市場對「高息持續更久」的預期令成長股估值承壓。防禦性板塊相對抗跌，體現出典型的避險情緒——資金從進取型資產撤離，流向較穩健的標的。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: A 'risk-off' environment is when investors become fearful and pull money out of volatile assets like tech stocks, moving it into safer havens like bonds, gold, or cash. You can spot it when the NASDAQ falls faster than the broader S&P 500, as happened today. In risk-off conditions, new stock positions are generally avoided because the tide is going out and even good companies can be dragged lower.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「避險情緒」（Risk-Off）是指投資者因市場恐慌而從高波動資產（如科技股）撤資，轉入債券、黃金或現金等安全資產的市場狀態。今日納斯達克跌幅遠超標普500，正是這種情緒的典型表現。在避險環境下，輕易開新倉風險較高——因為大盤向下時，即使是優質個股也難免受到拖累。
